skill.name: iacuc-protocol-drafter
skill.description: Draft IACUC protocol applications with structured 3Rs principles justification for animal research ethics review.
skill.summary: Automates drafting IACUC animal research protocol applications with detailed Replacement, Reduction, and Refinement justification sections.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 74 patterns across 4 files (974 lines). After AI evaluation, 70 of 74 findings are false positives: 33 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags triggered by the word 'protocol' in IACUC context (animal research, not cryptography), 36 'backtick execution' flags from markdown code formatting in documentation, and 3 'path traversal' flags from a security checklist warning against traversal. Two legitimate low-risk findings remain: a file write operation from user-supplied path (scripts/main.py:393) and a hardcoded reference URL (SKILL.md:7). No malicious intent, data exfiltration, code injection, or prompt injection detected. The skill is a straightforward IACUC protocol text generator using only Python standard library modules.

low_findings.title: File write from user-supplied output path
low_findings.description: The script writes generated protocol text to a file path specified by the user via the --output argument. While this is legitimate functionality for a document generator, the path is not validated against a restricted directory, which could allow writing to arbitrary locations.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a hardcoded GitHub URL pointing to the source repository. This is a reference link for attribution purposes only. The Python script makes no network calls and uses only standard library modules (json, argparse, sys, datetime, typing). No API keys or secrets are embedded.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Markdown backticks misidentified as shell command execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 36 backtick characters in SKILL.md and references/audit-reference.md as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All occurrences are standard markdown code spans and fenced code blocks used for documentation formatting. The Python script contains no subprocess, os.system, or shell execution calls.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: 'Protocol' keyword misidentified as weak cryptographic algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 33 occurrences of alleged weak cryptographic algorithms. The word 'protocol' in this skill refers to IACUC animal research protocols, not cryptographic protocols. No cryptographic code, hash functions, or encryption exists anywhere in the codebase.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Security checklist item misidentified as path traversal
dangerous_patterns.description: Lines 172 and 175 of SKILL.md contain a security checklist that explicitly warns against path traversal patterns ('No unauthorized file system access (../)' and 'Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)'). The static analyzer flagged the security control text itself as a vulnerability.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Audit file paths misidentified as system reconnaissance
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 10 locations as system reconnaissance. These are file paths in the audit result JSON and documented validation commands (py_compile, --help) in SKILL.md. No actual system reconnaissance or information gathering code exists.

dangerous_patterns.title: File write from user-supplied output path
dangerous_patterns.description: The script writes generated protocol text to a file path specified via --output argument without restricting the target directory. This is inherent to the skill's document generation purpose but represents a filesystem write risk that should be mitigated through sandboxed execution.

content.user_title: Draft IACUC Protocol Applications with 3Rs Justification
content.value_statement: Writing IACUC animal research protocols requires detailed 3Rs principle justifications that are time-consuming to prepare. This skill automates drafting complete IACUC protocol applications with structured Replacement, Reduction, and Refinement sections based on your experimental parameters.
actual_capabilities: Generate complete IACUC protocol applications from structured JSON input with all required sections
Produce detailed 3Rs justification sections covering Replacement, Reduction, and Refinement principles
Include humane endpoint definitions, veterinary care plans, and euthanasia method descriptions
Validate required input fields before generation and report missing data clearly
Provide sample input data via --sample flag for quick testing and format familiarization
Support configurable output templates including standard, minimal, and detailed formats
limitations: Generated drafts require review by a qualified researcher before formal IACUC submission
Output format may need adjustment to match specific institutional requirements and local regulations
Cannot verify the scientific accuracy of user-provided experimental parameters or sample size calculations
Requires structured JSON input and cannot generate protocols from unstructured natural language descriptions

use_cases.title: Principal Investigator Drafting New Protocols
use_cases.description: Researchers preparing initial IACUC submissions for new animal studies can generate complete draft protocols with all required sections from their experimental parameters, saving hours of manual writing.

use_cases.title: Research Administrator Standardizing Submissions
use_cases.description: IACUC office staff can use the skill to ensure consistent 3Rs justification formatting across protocol submissions from different laboratories, reducing review time and improving compliance.

use_cases.title: Graduate Student Learning Protocol Writing
use_cases.description: Students new to animal research can generate example protocols to understand the structure, required sections, and expected level of detail in IACUC applications before writing their own.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Protocol Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an IACUC protocol for a mouse study with 40 animals. The study tests a new drug effect on tumor growth. Use pain category C. Include all standard sections.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick protocol draft for a simple experimental design with minimal customization needed

prompt_templates.title: Focused 3Rs Justification
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft an IACUC 3Rs justification for a rat study involving surgical procedures. I need detailed Replacement alternatives with literature search documentation, statistical sample size calculation for Reduction using G*Power methodology, and comprehensive pain management protocols for Refinement.
prompt_templates.scenario: Detailed 3Rs section needed for a protocol with invasive procedures requiring strong ethical justification

prompt_templates.title: Full Protocol from JSON Input
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete IACUC protocol application from this JSON input. Use the detailed template and include all sections: header with institution info, project summary, 3Rs justification, animal procedures, veterinary care plan, humane endpoints, and references with declaration block.
prompt_templates.scenario: Production use with structured input data for a complete submission-ready draft

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Species Comparative Protocol
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an IACUC protocol for a multi-species comparative study using mice and zebrafish. For each species provide separate 3Rs justification sections with species-specific humane endpoints and enriched housing descriptions. Include a comparison table of endpoints across species. Output in markdown format.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex protocol requiring species-specific ethical considerations and comparative documentation

output_examples.input: Generate a basic IACUC protocol for a mouse behavioral study with 30 animals using pain category B
output_examples.output: A structured IACUC protocol document containing: header with institution name and application date, project summary listing species and procedure overview, complete 3Rs justification with literature search documentation across databases, animal procedures section covering origin and preparation, veterinary care plan with daily monitoring schedule, humane endpoint criteria with major and minor indicators, and a signature declaration block.

output_examples.input: Create an IACUC protocol with emphasis on the Reduction principle justification for a 60-animal tumor study
output_examples.output: A protocol with expanded Reduction section including: detailed sample size calculation using G*Power methodology showing effect size of 0.8, alpha of 0.05, and power of 0.8, justification for each statistical parameter, minimization strategies such as repeated measures design and historical control data sharing, and a step-by-step calculation table showing how the final group sizes were determined.

output_examples.input: Draft a refinement-focused IACUC protocol for a surgical study in rats requiring postoperative care
output_examples.output: A protocol with comprehensive Refinement section covering: pre-surgical anesthesia plan with drug names and administration routes, intraoperative monitoring procedures for anesthesia depth, postoperative analgesia schedule with specific drugs and dosing intervals, environmental enrichment specifications including cage dimensions and social housing arrangements, detailed humane endpoint criteria organized by major and minor indicators, and a monitoring frequency table spanning the full recovery period.

best_practices: Always verify generated protocol content against your institution's specific IACUC requirements and local animal welfare regulations before submission
Provide detailed input JSON with complete justification fields including alternatives considered, sample size methodology, and pain management specifics for the most thorough output
Use the sample input generator with the --sample flag first to understand the expected data structure and field requirements before creating your own input file
anti_patterns: Do not submit generated protocols to IACUC without thorough expert review of scientific accuracy, animal counts, and procedural details
Do not use the skill to generate protocols for experimental procedures that have not yet been approved by your institution's IACUC office or animal ethics committee
Do not rely on generated humane endpoint criteria as your sole guidance without consultation from a qualified laboratory animal veterinarian

faq.question: What are the 3Rs principles in animal research?
faq.answer: The 3Rs stand for Replacement (using non-animal alternatives when possible), Reduction (minimizing the number of animals used), and Refinement (reducing pain and distress). These principles are the ethical foundation of modern animal research and are required in all IACUC protocol applications.

faq.question: What input format does this skill require?
faq.answer: The skill accepts a JSON file with fields for experiment title, principal investigator, institution, species, number of animals, procedure description, pain category, and detailed justification data for each of the 3Rs principles. Use the --sample flag to see the complete format.

faq.question: Can this skill generate protocols for any animal species?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill accepts any species name in the input JSON. You should customize species-specific details such as housing requirements, humane endpoints, and veterinary care in the generated draft to match the actual species used in your study.

faq.question: Does this skill replace formal IACUC committee review?
faq.answer: No. Generated protocols are drafts to help you start the writing process. All protocols must still go through your institution's formal IACUC review and approval process. The skill saves time on drafting but does not substitute for ethical review.

faq.question: Can I submit the generated protocol text without modification?
faq.answer: You should carefully review and customize all generated content. Verify scientific accuracy, adjust formatting to match your institution's templates, confirm compliance with local and national regulations, and ensure all procedural details match your actual experimental plan.

faq.question: What software dependencies does this skill require?
faq.answer: The skill requires Python 3.8 or later. It uses only the Python standard library with no third-party package dependencies, so no pip install or package management is needed.
